titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/21

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=281 status=live modified=2017-09-12 15:00:01.917278+00 | translation moa=280 status=live modified=2017-03-24 15:55:11.251913+00

div.p:
Omnibus presens scriptum uisuris uel audituris  dauid Marescallus domini regis scottorum . salutem . Nouerit
vniuersitas uestra me dedisse et concessisse . et hac mea carta confirmasse Willelmo Lunnoc et heredibus
suis piscarias meas de Thueda . scilicet totam piscariam meam de holdema’ . et vnum rethe
super schipwel . quas Iocelinus lunnoc pater predicti Willelmi tenuit . Tenendas de me . et de heredibus meis
sibi et heredibus suis libere et quiete ab omni seruitio . et consuetudine . et exactione . reddendo singulis
annis viginti solidos . scilicet . vnam medietatem ad pentecosten . et aliam medietatem ad festum sancti mar
tini . Hiis testibus . Comite patricio . Willelmo cvmin . Waltero de lindes’ . Rogero filio henrici . Willelmo de Bernham . Will
elmo de fifle . Willelmo de salle . Alexander de fifle . Willelmo miriild . patricio filio Willelmi de fifle . Ricardo luuedar . Ricardo
filio Anke . bertolfo de hilton . Ricardo bernake . Willelmo guthered . Waltero brand . Ricardo clerico . et multis aliis .

To all who shall view or hear the present deed, David, marischal of the lord king of Scots, sends Greeting: Let your community know that I have given and granted and by this my charter made firm to Willliam Lunnock and his heirs my fisheries on the Tweed, that is, my whole fishery at ‘Holdema’, and one net on the Shipwell, which Jocelin Lunnock, the father of the aforesaid William, held. For him and his heirs to hold of me and my heirs free and quit from all service and custom and exaction by rendering 20 shillings each year, that is, a half at Pentecost and the other half at the feast of Saint Martin. With these witnesses, Earl Patrick, William Comyn, Walter of Lindsay, Roger son of Henry, William of Bernham, William of Fishley, William de Salle, Alexander of Fishley, William Miriild, Patrick son of William of Fishley, Richard Lauder, Richard son of Anke, Bertolf of Hilton, Richard Bernake, William Guthred, Walter Brand, Richard the clerk, and many others.
